title: The Webhook Replay
alert: Provider retries are replaying the same fulfillment event.

briefing.objective: Prevent a webhook retry from creating duplicate fulfillment work while continuing to accept new event IDs.
briefing.successCriterion: The Vitest acceptance suite must show exactly one processed event for each event ID after replay.
briefing.rootCause: The ingestion ledger records every delivery instead of recognizing an event that has already been processed.
briefing.evidence: The decisive check replays one provider event, then compares the processed-event ledger with the expected unique IDs.

telemetry.impact: Fulfillment has started duplicate packing jobs after the provider redelivered successful webhooks.

events.message: Processed fulfillment events exceeded unique provider event IDs.
evt-443 was redelivered after the provider timed out waiting for an acknowledgement.
Two packing jobs reference the same provider event.

stakeholderMessages.author: Maya - PM
stakeholderMessages.body: Operations paused the duplicate packing jobs. Can we contain replays without losing a new delivery?

stakeholderMessages.author: Jon - Senior Eng
stakeholderMessages.body: A provider retry is normal. Which identifier tells us that this delivery has already produced work?

stakeholderMessages.author: AI Pair
stakeholderMessages.body: I found three plausible edits. I would trace what the ledger represents before choosing a change.
